title: gilbertiodendron straussianum
intro: Gilbertiodendron straussianum is a species of tree in the family Sapotaceae, native to the tropical forests of Cameroon and Equatorial Guinea. It is also known as the African bread tree due to its starchy trunk that can be used as a food source. The bark and leaves of Gilbertiodendron straussianum have been used in traditional medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving pain. Herbal preparations of Gilbertiodendron straussianum can be made by infusing the leaves or bark in hot water or using the starchy trunk to produce a flour for baking, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy.
